data.title: Create CI portability smoke test workflow
data.description:
## Description

Create .github/workflows/portability-smoke.yml that runs .claude/scripts/dso ticket list in a clean Ubuntu container with no prior DSO configuration. This validates that the shim's sentinel self-detection works in a real fresh environment (SC7).

### Workflow spec:
- Name: "Portability Smoke Test"
- Triggers: push to main, pull_request to main
- Runner: ubuntu-latest
- Steps:
  1. actions/checkout@v4 (full clone with fetch-depth: 0 to get all branches)
  2. Set up ticket system: git worktree add .tickets-tracker tickets
  3. Run: .claude/scripts/dso ticket list
  4. Assert exit 0 (the run step itself validates this)
- No CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT env var set, no special config — relies on sentinel self-detection

### TDD exemption
Unit test exempt. Justification: CI workflow behavior is only validatable by executing in GitHub Actions infrastructure; unit tests can only verify the file is parseable YAML, which is covered by the Verify: command using python3/yaml.safe_load. This meets infrastructure-boundary-only criterion (criterion #3).

### File Impact
- .github/workflows/portability-smoke.yml (create)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] bash tests/run-all.sh passes (exit 0)
  Verify: bash tests/run-all.sh
- [ ] .github/workflows/portability-smoke.yml exists
  Verify: test -f .github/workflows/portability-smoke.yml
- [ ] Workflow YAML is valid (parseable)
  Verify: python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/portability-smoke.yml'))"
- [ ] Workflow runs dso ticket list command
  Verify: grep -q 'dso ticket list' .github/workflows/portability-smoke.yml
- [ ] Workflow uses ubuntu-latest
  Verify: grep -q 'ubuntu-latest' .github/workflows/portability-smoke.yml
- [ ] Workflow does full clone (fetch-depth: 0)
  Verify: grep -q 'fetch-depth.*0' .github/workflows/portability-smoke.yml
